homeError: Couldn’t load your home. Try again later.
homeNoPlan: your coach hasn’t assigned you a plan yet
homeLinkCuenta: Link up with your coach in Account ›
homeCheckinDoneSub: Thanks for logging · you can edit it whenever
titularNoneMsg: There are no logs yet to read your state. Your first check-in starts building your baseline — with no history, we don’t make up a state.
titularOkMsg: Your recovery is in your normal range. Keep following the plan.
titularWarnMsg: Your recovery is a bit below your normal. Today drop the intensity a notch and prioritize sleep.
titularAlertMsg: Your recovery is well below your normal. Today it’s best to ease off — talk to your coach.
tipFooter: {source} · educational material, not a replacement for your coach
caminoNoPlanTitle: You don’t have a plan assigned yet
caminoNoPlanBody: When your coach assigns you a macrocycle, you’ll see your road to the next competition here.
caminoSub: macro track · each bar = one week · gold outline = competition
semAllDone: ✓ You logged the whole week — tap a day to edit.
semTapRegister: tap a day to log your workout
semInOrder: Complete the days in order to continue.
semWeekTotal: This week: {kg} kg · {done}/{total} {count, plural, one {session} other {sessions}}
ciDoneBody: We logged your day. This feeds your trend — not a score to compete on. Come back tomorrow, no pressure.
ciWeightQ: How much do you weigh today?
enBlockedRegister: Complete the previous day before logging this one.
enAnularConfirm: Skip this workout? It’s marked as skipped (no volume). You can reactivate it later.
enBlockedAnular: Complete the previous day before skipping this one.
enLockedMsg: Complete the previous day to unlock this one.
enAnuladoMsg: This workout is skipped (you skipped it). It adds no volume.
fsTitleConflicto: You already logged a workout today
fsTitleEditar: When did you do this workout?
fsSubConflicto: This log needs its own date — when did you do it?
fsSubEditar: The log keeps the real workout date.
fsTaken: That date already has a logged workout.
fsOutOfWeek: Heads up: it falls outside the plan week — allowed anyway.
saWarmupFirst: Warm up first — prep your body for the heavy weight.
saSublead: Tap a set to mark it · adjust with ✎
saFinishMeta: {exDone}/{exTotal} {exTotal, plural, one {exercise} other {exercises}} · {setsDone}/{setsTotal} {setsTotal, plural, one {set done} other {sets done}}
wuHint: {count} {count, plural, one {done} other {done}} · they hide when marked
heroMeta: {sets} {sets, plural, one {set} other {sets}} × {reps} {reps, plural, one {rep} other {reps}} · per side · bar {barKg} kg
vicNoSetsMarked: — no sets marked. When you mark your sets, we'll celebrate them.
vicWeekMeta: {count, plural, one {# session logged} other {# sessions logged}}
vicMacroMeta: {count, plural, one {# week · ready to compete} other {# weeks · ready to compete}}
celStreak: {count, plural, one {week} other {weeks}} streak · rest doesn't break it
celRadarEmpty: Log your day to day and here you'll see today's wellness vs your average.
celKgSeries: {kg} kg · {count, plural, one {# set} other {# sets}}
radAriaTodayVsAvg: Your wellness today vs your average
cicGateIntro: Your cycle is yours. If you activate it, you project its windows onto YOUR plan calendar and can —if you want— share an always-redacted context with your coach. It's not a traffic light and doesn't change your plan: it just gives context.
cicAckLabel: I understand this does not replace the advice of a health professional. More detail in the <privacy>privacy policy</privacy>.
cicRegistryIntro: Your cycle is yours. Logging it projects its windows onto YOUR plan calendar; sharing with your coach is separate and always redacted.
cicShareNoneDesc: The coach sees nothing about the cycle.
cicShareMinDesc: The coach only knows that you log (reliability and health) — no detail.
cicShareFullDesc: Also sees whether you are in a luteal window TODAY. Never date, phase or symptoms.
cicStateUnreliableNote: With an irregular cycle we don't project windows (it would be false precision). Logging still adds context.
cicStateAmenorrheaNote: No period for months: it's worth discussing with a health professional. It's not an athletic achievement.
cicLenError: Must be an integer between {min} and {max}.
cicHorizonNote: Your start date is more than {count, plural, one {# cycle} other {# cycles}} old — update it so the projection returns to your calendar.
cicRevokeNote: Deactivating deletes your cycle log and your coach stops seeing any context.
ccarEmpty: Log your cycle in Account (regular + last period date + duration) to see it charted here.
progSubtitle: your trends vs your own normal
progLoadError: Couldn't load your progress. Try again later.
progSignalRecuperacionSub: HRV and resting HR vs your normal
progSignalBienestarSub: score 0–100 vs your normal · your items
progExplainCargaForma: Each cell is a day; the color rises with the volume (kg of work) you moved that day. Days without training stay gray.
progExplainCargaSirve: See your load day by day and week by week, against your own baseline.
progExplainCargaLectura: Streaks of very intense days in a row without rest are a sign to look after recovery.
progExplainRecuperacionForma: Each cell is a day; the color follows your HRV for that week of the macro (higher = better recovery). Outside the macro it stays gray.
progExplainRecuperacionSirve: Read how you're recovering, beyond how you trained.
progExplainRecuperacionLectura: Within your band, you're doing fine. HRV dropping or resting HR rising for several weeks = time to ease off.
progExplainBienestarForma: Each cell is a day; the color rises with your wellness score (0–100) for that day — the average of your 6 check-in items.
progExplainBienestarSirve: See how you've been feeling, beyond the physiological numbers.
progExplainBienestarLectura: It's read against your own normal; days without a check-in stay gray.
progExplainPesoForma: Each cell is a day; the closer to your category (in band), the more intense the color.
progExplainPesoSirve: Track whether you're at your category weight heading into the competition.
progExplainPesoLectura: The band is your category limits; days without weighing in stay gray.
progNoDataBody: When you log HRV, HR, and load, your trends appear here — with the context of how to read each chart.
pmapContextPeriodo: Period (projection from your log) — context, not a rule.
pmapContextPreperiodo: Pre-period (projection from your log) — context, not a rule.
pmapDaySub: Session {n}{hasDen, select, yes { of {den}} other {}}{hasTop, select, yes { · top {top}%} other {}}
pmapProjectionExplain: Projection assuming {len}-day cycles from your last date — period = first {periodDays} days, pre-period = last {preDays}; it turns off after {horizon, plural, one {# cycle} other {# cycles}} without updating.
pmapCollision: Your heaviest week (W{week}) lands in your {kind, select, periodo {period} other {pre-period}} window (projection).
padTypicalTrainingLook: What a typical session looks like
padPlanWeekNote: Week {week} of the plan — your kg come from your current maxes.
padWhatItMeansExplain: It's the percentage of your maxes (1RM) the bars move in this phase. Your coach calculates it from your recorded 1RMs. The band is the range the plan expects here; if your training falls outside, your coach reviews it.
padInPhaseWeek: You're in week {week} of this phase{left, plural, =0 { — last week} other { — {left} left}}.
